title: Authenticated Gateway to Protected Backend
triggers: requests must pass auth before reaching the backend
protect internal apis with authentication and authorization

requiredNodes.label: Client
requiredNodes.description: Caller attempting to access protected functionality.

requiredNodes.label: Auth Gateway
requiredNodes.description: Gateway that authenticates and authorizes inbound traffic.

requiredNodes.label: Protected Service
requiredNodes.description: Backend service reachable only after access checks pass.

requiredNodes.label: Protected Datastore
requiredNodes.description: Restricted persistence layer for protected business data.

optionalNodes.label: Identity Provider
optionalNodes.description: External or shared service that verifies identities and issues claims.

optionalNodes.label: Policy Store
optionalNodes.description: Configuration store for authorization rules and policies.

invariants.rule: Protected Service traffic must pass through the Auth Gateway before execution.
invariants.rationale: The gateway enforces the auth boundary that defines this pattern.

invariants.rule: The Protected Service must interact with the Protected Datastore only after access checks are completed.
invariants.rationale: Authorization should be resolved before protected state is read or mutated.

invariants.rule: If authorization failure is described explicitly, a denied or blocked outcome should also be modeled.
invariants.rationale: Security flows are clearer when both allowed and denied paths are visible.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt focuses on token issuance flows with an identity provider and client redirect behavior.
antiPatterns.rationale: That is closer to an explicit authn/authz token flow than a gateway-protected backend.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt is primarily about fan-out, background workers, or event consumers.
antiPatterns.rationale: Security gating is secondary in those topologies.

clarifications.question: Should the gateway only authorize access, or should it also integrate with an identity provider?
clarifications.reason: Identity federation may require adding a dedicated identity component.

clarifications.question: Do you want denied requests represented explicitly in the diagram?
clarifications.reason: Security review diagrams often need both allow and deny outcomes.
